chapter_title.en: Chapter on What Has Been Reported Regarding One Who Kills and Then Flees

text: 5379 - رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ فَضَّالٍ عَنْ ظَرِيفِ بْنِ نَاصِحٍ عَنْ أَبَانِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ قَتَلَ رَجُلاً عَمْداً ثُمَّ فَرَّ فَلَمْ يُقْدَرْ عَلَيْهِ حَتَّى مَاتَ قَالَ "إِنْ كَانَ لَهُ مَالٌ أُخِذَ مِنْهُ وَ إِلاَّ أُخِذَ مِنَ اَلْأَقْرَبِ فَالْأَقْرَبِ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5379 - Al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Faddal narrated from Zharif ibn Nasih, from Aban ibn Uthman, from Abu Basir, who narrated from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) regarding a man who intentionally killed another man and then fled, and he could not be captured until he died.
Imam (as) said: "If he had wealth, the blood money is taken from it. If he had no wealth, it is taken from his closest relatives, and then from those next closest to him."
